goal: Prevent contract-reality mismatches from reaching the Composer loop by introducing dual-form acceptance criteria, a shared OPERATING_CONTRACT.md rendered into both agent prompts, a structured @contract-deviation marker protocol, and a new Validator sub-agent invoked at tap-into authoring time.
constraints: Dual-form criterion shape: { behavioral: string, mechanism: Option<string> }. Behavioral is the load-bearing obligation the Composer must satisfy; mechanism is the suggested implementation path, advisory and substitutable under OPERATING_CONTRACT.md rules. Reviewer validates behavioral first, then mechanism.

Schema migration is strict in the final state. The union that permits legacy single-string criteria is transient — used only during S1 to keep the running contract loadable through the transition. By end of S1.T3 all three .tap/features/*/FEATURE_CONTRACT.json files are dual-form only.

.tap/prompts/OPERATING_CONTRACT.md is rendered into BOTH Composer and Reviewer prompts via ContextEngine. Contains shared rules (never --no-verify, never git push, scope discipline) and the @contract-deviation JSON marker wire-protocol spec.

Substitution marker is a JSON block-comment emitted at the substitution site. Required fields: criterion_ref, invalidity (tsc-error | criterion-conflict | global-constraint-conflict), evidence, substitution, behavioral_preserved_ref. Any format deviation is a Reviewer FAIL.

Validator sub-agent is invoked via Agent(subagent_type="Validator") from within tap-into's main-thread Claude Code session — one-level nest, not an OS subprocess. Validator writes VALIDATION_RESULT.md to the draft feature directory.

Validator check categories: structural (schema decode, dep cycle, dangling ids, missing fields), path-reality (git ls-files / check-ignore on each prescribed path), self-contradiction (criterion-pair text scan), testability heuristic (behavioral names a file, command output, or observable), mechanism-viability (stack-dispatched).

Mechanism-viability stack-dispatch for v0: TypeScript (scratch file + bunx tsc --noEmit, grep node_modules) and Rust (scratch file + cargo check, grep ~/.cargo/registry/src/). Unknown stacks degrade to LLM-only prose with warning severity. Multi-stack expansion out of scope per TODO in .claude/skills/tap-into/SKILL.md.

VALIDATION_RESULT.md findings split by severity: blocker halts tap-into emission until fixed; warning permits emission and is forwarded to Composer and Reviewer prompts via ContextEngine so both agents see flagged criteria upfront.

Composer skill update uses positive framing: 'your obligation is the behavioral field; treat the mechanism field as the suggested implementation path.' No negative-phrased prohibitions on touching the behavioral field (pink-elephant rule).

Reviewer skill update: per-criterion dual-form classification (behavioral and mechanism classified independently). On @contract-deviation marker: parse JSON, reproduce claimed invalidity with prescribed mechanism in a scratch file, confirm behavioral_preserved_ref is Satisfied. If reproduction shows literal mechanism works, FAIL with 'false invalidity claim'.

The loop-runner constraint-21 contract-reality escape hatch remains in force. Validator operates upstream at authoring time; execution-time substitutions via markers remain legitimate for Validator blind spots, package-version drift, and new failure modes arising between authoring and execution.

Bootstrappability: every task must be buildable under the state existing when it starts. S1 splits into schema-lenient → migrate-all-contracts → schema-strict so the contract being run is always loadable through the transition.
Tests in sibling __tests__/ folders per CLAUDE.md. No any, no as unknown as. Branded construction imports the existing brand<B>(s) helper from src/services/brand.ts.
scripts/bootstrap.ts remains the entry point until tap run CLI lands. No CLI work in this feature.
